Title: The complex jujube genome provides insights into fruit tree biology

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression of most MFS sugar transporter genes increases during fruit ripening
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression of most MFS sugar transporter genes increases during fruit ripening
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stages: young, white mature, half-red, full red
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome annotation plus RNA-seq expression profiling correlated with sugar measurements across fruit development
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely supports transport-mediated sugar accumulation in ripening jujube fruit
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Sucrose becomes the dominant sugar in mature fruit. 2. Most MFS sugar transporter genes show enhanced expression with fruit ripening. 3. The authors infer that sugar transport from phloem into fruit helps determine final sugar accumulation.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing; RNA-seq from 15 tissues; fruit sugar metabolite analysis by HPLC (accession NA)
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper treats this as a gene family rather than naming specific transporter loci.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate transporter family for improving fruit sugar loading and sweetness

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts GDP-D-mannose to GDP-L-galactose/GDP-L-gulose in the L-galactose ascorbate biosynthesis pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Continuously highly expressed during fruit development/ripening
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Continuously highly expressed during fruit development/ripening
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stages: young, white mature, half-red, full red
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome annotation plus RNA-seq expression profiling during fruit development with comparative pathway analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to high vitamin C accumulation in jujube fruit
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GME is one of the two key enzymes highlighted in the L-galactose pathway. 2. Its transcripts remained continuously high during fruit development. 3. The data support the L-galactose pathway as the major route for AsA accumulation in jujube fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing; RNA-seq from 15 tissues (accession NA)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper analyzes gene family/pathway level rather than reporting a single specific locus identifier for GME.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering elevated vitamin C in fruit crops, especially with recycling-pathway genes

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: HAD-like sugar phosphatase-associated GDP-L-galactose phosphorylase family
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts GDP-L-galactose to L-galactose-1-phosphate in the committed/central steps of the L-galactose pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms precursor toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Continuously highly expressed during fruit development/ripening
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Continuously highly expressed during fruit development/ripening
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stages: young, white mature, half-red, full red
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome-wide comparative analysis, positive selection analysis, and RNA-seq expression profiling during fruit development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly associated with extreme vitamin C accumulation in jujube fruit
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GGP is highlighted as a key enzyme in the major L-galactose AsA biosynthetic pathway. 2. It shows continuously high expression during fruit development. 3. Comparative genomics identified GGP as a positively selected gene in jujube.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing; RNA-seq from 15 tissues (accession NA)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified by comparative genomics as positively selected in jujube.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for vitamin C biofortification in fruit trees

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces monodehydroascorbate back to ascorbate using NADH/NADPH in the ascorbate recycling pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for regeneration of target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Continuously highly expressed during fruit development/ripening
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Continuously highly expressed during fruit development/ripening
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stages: young, white mature, half-red, full red
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative genomics, phylogenetic analysis of gene family expansion, genomic localization, and RNA-seq expression profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to regeneration and maintenance of high vitamin C levels in jujube fruit
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MDHAR is highlighted as the key enzyme in AsA recycling in jujube. 2. MDHAR genes are significantly expanded in the jujube genome, including eight tandemly duplicated copies in one subfamily. 3. MDHAR transcripts remain continuously high during fruit development, supporting a major role in AsA regeneration.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing; RNA-seq from 15 tissues (accession NA)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Subfamilies IV and V show lineage-specific patterns; subfamily V contains eight jujube copies likely from tandem duplication.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important stacking target with biosynthetic genes to enhance vitamin C through both synthesis and recycling
